title: marrubium globosum subsp. globosum
intro: Marrubium globosum subsp. globosum, also known as globe-leaved horehound, is a perennial plant in the mint family that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as respiratory problems and digestive issues. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and soothing gastrointestinal upset, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Globe-leaved horehound can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
